title: Upon The Roses In Julia's Bosom.
content:
Thrice happy roses, so much grac'd to have
    Within the bosom of my love your grave.
    Die when ye will, your sepulchre is known,
    Your grave her bosom is, the lawn the stone.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "Upon The Roses In Julia's Bosom."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
